---
title: "Woodland False Buttonweed (Spermacoce remota)"
slug: "spermacoce-remota"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/476002936/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Woodland False Buttonweed"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Gentianales"
tax_family: "Rubiaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Gentianales", "Rubiaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/476002936/medium.jpg" alt="Centipede Grass Orchid" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/lmcconachie">
lmcconachie</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/265046717">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Gentianales
-   **Family:** Rubiaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=326037)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in shaded, moist habitats.

## Natural history notes

A small herb characterized by its opposite leaves and clusters of tiny white flowers at the leaf nodes. In Florida, it is commonly found in shaded, moist habitats such as forests, hammocks, and along trails. It grows during the warmer months and often forms low patches in understory vegetation.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/326037-Spermacoce-remota)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/genus/1153)
